title: SDAT
wikitext:
[[File:SDAT.jpg|thumb|Shinji's SDAT player]]

'''SDAT''' (or ''S-DAT'') is a format of ''Digital Audio Tape'' developed by Sony. The "S" prefix denotes that it is the format which uses a stationary magnetic head. [[Shinji Ikari]] is seen throughout the [[Neon Genesis Evangelion]] TV Series and Movies listening to an SDAT player, noteably switching between tracks 25 and 26. Some fans have postulated that there is a possible connection between [[FGC:Episode 04 Cut 042|tracks 25 and 26 and the last 2 episodes of the TV Series]]. 

The tracks are:<ref>[http://animeboards.com/showthread.php?t=49863 What is that song Shinji listens to?] ''Animeboards.com Forums''. 2003-01-31. Retrieved 2016-07-01.</ref>

Episode 2 - A-2 ("Background Music" - S² Works, Disc-01, track 02)<ref>[https://www.discogs.com/Shiro-Sagisu-neon-Genesis-Evangelion-S%C2%B2-Works/release/8649787 Shiro Sagisu - neon Genesis Evangelion: S² Works (CD, Album) at Discogs] ''Discogs''. 2016-06-15. Retrieved 2016-07-01.</ref> and B-4 ("Fly Me To the Moon" - S² Works, Disc-01, track 27)

Episode 4 - "Blue Legend"<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YbtnN7RETzk Lilia ~from Ys~ - Blue Legend - YouTube] Jun 25, 2015 - Uploaded by Falcom Music Channel</ref> (蒼いレジェンド ''Aoi Legend''), track 3 of  Lilia ~from Ys~<ref>[http://vgmdb.net/album/4584 KICA-132 | Lilia~from Ys~ - VGMdb] ''VGMdb''. Retrieved 2016-07-01.</ref> and "You are the only one"<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NA8_vMOqOEs Lilia ~from Ys~ - You are the only one - YouTube] Jun 25, 2015 - Uploaded by Falcom Music Channel</ref>, track 1 of Lilia ~from Ys~

Episode 9 - "Blue Legend" (蒼いレジェンド ''Aoi Legend'') and "Distant Empty Promise"<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pFInGqUAtsE Lilia ~from Ys~ - Distant Empty Promise - YouTube] Jun 25, 2015 - Uploaded by Falcom Music Channel</ref> (遠い空の約束 ''Tôi Sora no Yakusoku''), track 8 of Lilia ~from Ys~

Episode 15 - "You are the only one", track 1 of Lilia ~from Ys~

Episode 23 - "Ryoute Ippai no Yume" (両手いっぱいの夢 lit. Both Hands Full of Dreams), track 4 of Masami Okui ‎- Gyuu<ref>[https://www.discogs.com/Masami-Okui-Gyuu/release/7261356 Masami Okui - Gyuu (CD, Album) at Discogs] ''Discogs''. 2015-07-21. Retrieved 2016-07-01</ref>

Episode 24 - Ode to Joy - Beethoven

Episode 25' - It plays nothing...

==The SDAT in Rebuild==
{{Rebuildcontentstart}}
[[File:27.PNG|200px|thumb|SDAT player in Evangelion 2.0]]
In [[Evangelion 2.0: You Can (Not) Advance]], it is revealed that the SDAT player functions as a token of attachment to his father, [[Gendo Ikari]] and it's used to shut himself off the outside world. Shinji mentions that his SDAT player was belonged to Gendo, Shinji took it after it was left in his teacher's home. In on scene, the SDAT players skips from track 25 to 27 shortly before the plot diverges from that of the original series, lending credence to the theory that the track numbers are related to the episodes. In later part of the film, Shinji throws the SDAT player in a rubbish bin as he loses faith in his father. [[Rei#In_Rebuild_of_Evangelion|Rei]] subsequently picks it up and keeps it.

The SDAT player in Evangelion 2.0 and 3.0 has "Super･Digital Audio Tape" under SDAT. 

In [[Evangelion 3.0: You Can (Not) Redo]], track 28 is played. At the last part of the film, it's unknown [[Guides:Evangelion_3.33_FAQ#Did_Rei_pick_up_the_SDAT_player_or_not.3F|did Rei pick up the SDAT player or not]].
{{Rebuildcontentend}}

==See Also==
*[[FGC:Supplemental (The Significance of Shinji's SDAT)|The Significance of Shinji's SDAT]]

html:
SDAT (or S-DAT) is a format of Digital Audio Tape developed by Sony. The "S" prefix denotes that it is the format which uses a stationary magnetic head. Shinji Ikari is seen throughout the Neon Genesis Evangelion TV Series and Movies listening to an SDAT player, noteably switching between tracks 25 and 26. Some fans have postulated that there is a possible connection between tracks 25 and 26 and the last 2 episodes of the TV Series.

The tracks are:[1]

Episode 2 - A-2 ("Background Music" - S² Works, Disc-01, track 02)[2] and B-4 ("Fly Me To the Moon" - S² Works, Disc-01, track 27)

Episode 4 - "Blue Legend"[3] (蒼いレジェンド Aoi Legend), track 3 of Lilia ~from Ys~[4] and "You are the only one"[5], track 1 of Lilia ~from Ys~

Episode 9 - "Blue Legend" (蒼いレジェンド Aoi Legend) and "Distant Empty Promise"[6] (遠い空の約束 Tôi Sora no Yakusoku), track 8 of Lilia ~from Ys~

Episode 15 - "You are the only one", track 1 of Lilia ~from Ys~

Episode 23 - "Ryoute Ippai no Yume" (両手いっぱいの夢 lit. Both Hands Full of Dreams), track 4 of Masami Okui ‎- Gyuu[7]

Episode 24 - Ode to Joy - Beethoven

Episode 25' - It plays nothing...

The SDAT in Rebuild[edit]

In Evangelion 2.0: You Can (Not) Advance, it is revealed that the SDAT player functions as a token of attachment to his father, Gendo Ikari and it's used to shut himself off the outside world. Shinji mentions that his SDAT player was belonged to Gendo, Shinji took it after it was left in his teacher's home. In on scene, the SDAT players skips from track 25 to 27 shortly before the plot diverges from that of the original series, lending credence to the theory that the track numbers are related to the episodes. In later part of the film, Shinji throws the SDAT player in a rubbish bin as he loses faith in his father. Rei subsequently picks it up and keeps it.

The SDAT player in Evangelion 2.0 and 3.0 has "Super･Digital Audio Tape" under SDAT.

In Evangelion 3.0: You Can (Not) Redo, track 28 is played. At the last part of the film, it's unknown did Rei pick up the SDAT player or not.

See Also[edit]

  • The Significance of Shinji's SDAT
  • Music in Chronological Order of Usage

References[edit]

  1. ↑ What is that song Shinji listens to? Animeboards.com Forums. 2003-01-31. Retrieved 2016-07-01.
  2. ↑ Shiro Sagisu - neon Genesis Evangelion: S² Works (CD, Album) at Discogs Discogs. 2016-06-15. Retrieved 2016-07-01.
  3. ↑ Lilia ~from Ys~ - Blue Legend - YouTube Jun 25, 2015 - Uploaded by Falcom Music Channel
  4. ↑ KICA-132 | Lilia~from Ys~ - VGMdb VGMdb. Retrieved 2016-07-01.
  5. ↑ Lilia ~from Ys~ - You are the only one - YouTube Jun 25, 2015 - Uploaded by Falcom Music Channel
  6. ↑ Lilia ~from Ys~ - Distant Empty Promise - YouTube Jun 25, 2015 - Uploaded by Falcom Music Channel
  7. ↑ Masami Okui - Gyuu (CD, Album) at Discogs Discogs. 2015-07-21. Retrieved 2016-07-01
